title: Notes to yourself
why.no-comment: There is no comment. Open one with <!-- and write whatever you like. Or comment out the second paragraph and watch it disappear.
why.not-closed-comment: You opened a comment and never closed it. --> is missing. And this one is dangerous: everything after it vanishes from the page, and you will have no idea why.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed.
blocks.0.text: Sometimes you want to leave a note in the code — for yourself in a month, or for whoever reads it after you. But you do not want it on the page.
blocks.2.text: Everything between <!-- and --> disappears. The browser skips it as if it were not there.
blocks.3.text: The second use is the more useful one
blocks.4.text: Not sure whether to keep a paragraph? Do not delete it — comment it out. If tomorrow you decide it was good, you remove the comment and get it back.
blocks.6.text: Try it on the page on the right: comment out the second paragraph and run. It disappears. Remove the comment, run again — it comes back.
blocks.8.text: A comment is not visible on the page, but it is in the code — and anyone can view the code of any page with two clicks. So it is not a secret. Never write anything there you would not want read.
